sentence: Subjective:

HPI: Ashley James, a 14-year-old female, presents with a history of abdominal pain and vomiting, initially diagnosed as a stomach virus, but later found to have an atrophic right kidney on ultrasound.
She has a history of recurrent infections and hospitalizations as a child, with a suspected severe infection that may have affected her kidney.
Her father had a kidney transplant and died at 47 due to dialysis.
Her aunt (grandfather's sister) has end-stage renal disease, chronic kidney disease, stage V, diabetes, and hypertension.
Ashley takes 40 mg of Pepcid daily for unknown indication and Claritin for allergies.
She has a family history of renal disease and breast cancer in her mother.
Past medical history: Ashley has a history of recurrent infections and hospitalizations as a child, with a suspected severe infection that may have affected her kidney.
Her father had a kidney transplant and died at 47 due to dialysis.
Her aunt (grandfather's sister) has end-stage renal disease, chronic kidney disease, stage V, diabetes, and hypertension.
Ashley's mother has breast cancer.
Review of systems: Ashley complains of abdominal pain and burning sensation when eating, especially with carbonated drinks.
She also mentions pain when starting to urinate.
Current medications: Pepcid 40 mg daily, Claritin (no dose mentioned).
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: Ashley appears well-appearing, alert, and oriented, with no acute distress.
Her lungs and heart sound great.
Her physical exam is within normal limits.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: Ashley has a history of recurrent infections and hospitalizations as a child, with a suspected severe infection that may have affected her kidney, leading to an atrophic right kidney.
She has a family history of renal disease and breast cancer.
Problem list: Atrophic right kidney; Recurrent infections; Suspected severe infection affecting kidney; Family history of renal disease; Family history of breast cancer.
Plan:
I recommended that Ashley have an ultrasound to monitor the size of her kidneys and their overall function annually.
I also recommended a urinalysis to continue to make sure that protein and blood are not in her pee, and a blood pressure check.
I advised against nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory medicines like ibuprofen and instead recommended Tylenol.
I recommended that Ashley stay well-hydrated by drinking at least 2 liters of water daily and maintain a healthy diet and active lifestyle to help keep her and her kidneys healthier as an adult.
Follow-up:
Ashley can meet with her primary care physician or an adult nephrologist closer to her home for her next appointment.
